jobs.title: Environmental Health Safety Specialist
jobs.description:
Company Description
Western University of Health Sciences is an independent, nonprofit health professions university with campuses in Pomona, California, and Lebanon, Oregon. Founded in 1977, the university focuses on graduate-level education across a wide spectrum of health disciplines. Programs include biotechnology and pharmaceutical sciences, dental medicine, medical sciences, nursing, optometry, osteopathic medicine, pharmacy, physical therapy, physician assistant studies, podiatric medicine, and veterinary medicine. WesternU emphasizes interdisciplinary collaboration, preparing future health professionals to deliver patient-centered, evidence-based care in diverse settings.
Role Description
The Environmental Health Safety Specialist is a full-time, on-site role based in Pomona, CA. This position is responsible for implementing and monitoring environmental health and safety (EHS) programs to ensure a safe campus environment for students, faculty, staff, and visitors. Day-to-day tasks include conducting safety inspections and risk assessments, supporting regulatory compliance, and maintaining records related to occupational health and workplace safety. The specialist develops and delivers safety training, assists with incident investigations, and recommends corrective actions to prevent future occurrences. This role collaborates with academic and administrative departments to promote a strong safety culture and continuous improvement in health and safety practices.
Qualifications

Strong foundation in Health & Safety and Occupational Health, with the ability to assess risks and implement appropriate controls.
Experience with Environment Health and Safety (EHS) programs, including regulatory compliance and environmental risk management.
Knowledge of Workplace Safety practices, including hazard identification, incident reporting, and emergency response procedures.
Ability to design and deliver effective Safety Training for diverse audiences, using clear communication and adult-learning principles.
Bachelor’s degree in environmental health, occupational safety, industrial hygiene, public health, or a related field; relevant certifications (e.g., CSP, CIH, CHMM) are a plus.
Familiarity with federal, state, and local safety regulations (e.g., OSHA, EPA) and experience in higher education, healthcare, or laboratory environments preferred.
Strong analytical, organizational, and documentation skills, with the ability to work collaboratively across departments and interact respectfully with individuals from diverse backgrounds.
Proficiency with standard office software and safety management systems for tracking inspections, training, and incident data.

Show more

Show less

Seniority level

Entry level

Employment type

Full-time

Job function

Management and Manufacturing

Industries

Higher Education
